title: pkg/specgen: properly identify image OS on FreeBSD
body:
When working on Linux emulation on FreeBSD, I assumed that SpecGenerator.ImageOS was always populated from the image's OS value but in fact, this value comes from the CLI --os flag if set, otherwise "". This broke running FreeBSD native containers unless --os=freebsd was also set. Fix the problem by getting the value from the image itself.

This is a strong incentive for me to complete a stalled project to enable podman system tests on FreeBSD.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: This needs `[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]` in commit message.

> This needs `[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]` in commit message.

Fixed, thanks!
The test failure seems to be a flake - the failing test passes when run manually on a fedora 37 VM

Perhaps my original plan of doing this in specgen/generate is better after all - as far as I can tell, that code is only executed server-side. I originally had this logic in SpecGenToOCI which is the only place which needs to know the image's OS value.

Originally, I wanted to get this value from the image itself and while trying to figure out how to do that, I came across the SpecGenerator.ImageOS field which in retrospect was the wrong thing altogether. I should be able to get the right value using libimage. I'll try that.

Are there any other concerns or comments for this one? I have tested both locally and with podman-remote on both FreeBSD and MacOS to verify that the right image is fetched when OS is not specified and that linux emulation is selected based on the image itself by using 'podman run <id of linux image> ...'
